# runner.run_checks { #pycmdcheck.runner.run_checks }

```python
runner.run_checks(
    package_path,
    checks=None,
    skip=None,
    config=None,
    parallel=True,
)
```

Run checks on a package.

This is the core function that orchestrates check discovery and execution.
It loads configuration, discovers available checks via entry points,
and runs them either in parallel or sequentially.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`) \| [Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory to check. Can be a string or Path object. Will be resolved to an absolute path.

<code>[**checks**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[str](`str`)\] \| None]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   List of specific check names to run. If None, runs all checks that are enabled in the configuration. Available built-in checks: metadata, structure, tests, linting, typing, imports, license, docs.

<code>[**skip**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[str](`str`)\] \| None]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   List of check names to skip, even if they would otherwise run.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\] \| None]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Pre-loaded configuration dictionary. If None, configuration is loaded from the package's pyproject.toml file.

<code>[**parallel**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Whether to run checks in parallel using threads. Defaults to True. Set to False for deterministic ordering or debugging.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Report](`pycmdcheck.results.Report`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A Report object containing all check results. The report includes

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Report](`pycmdcheck.results.Report`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   the package path and a list of CheckResult objects.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Run all enabled checks:

```python
>>> report = run_checks("/path/to/package")
>>> report.passed
True
```

Run specific checks only:

```python
>>> report = run_checks(".", checks=["metadata", "tests"])
>>> len(report.results)
2
```

Run sequentially for debugging:

```python
>>> report = run_checks(".", parallel=False)
```
